fragility_points: A volatility collapse or broader crypto pullback would reduce touch odds quickly.

main_logical_chain: Interpret the contract using Polymarket's rule text: Binance 1-minute ETH/USDT High is the source of truth.
Check current and recent Binance highs: price got close but remained below $2,400.
Cross-check spot context on Coinbase/CoinGecko/Binance: ETH is already within about 0.5% of the threshold.
Apply outside-view logic: multi-day crypto windows often produce nearby threshold touches, but not with 92% certainty when the touch has not yet occurred.
Conclude Yes is favored, but less strongly than the market implies.
payload.main_thesis: ETH reaching $2,400 by Apr 19 is more likely than not because it is already trading within roughly 0.5% of the threshold, but the market's 92.35% probability overstates certainty for a still-unrealized short-horizon crypto touch event.
quote_anchors: Market resolves Yes if any Binance 1-minute ETH/USDT candle ... has a final High >= $2,400.
payload.source_quality_view: High-quality source-of-truth interpretation from Polymarket rules plus direct Binance data; contextual spot cross-check is strong but only medium-independence.
strongest_disconfirmers: ETH had still not actually printed $2,400 on the governing Binance metric at check time despite getting close already.

strongest_supports: Checked spot across Binance, Coinbase, and CoinGecko was about $2,387-$2,389, only $11-$13 below the threshold.
Binance recent highs reached 2396.03, showing the market already came within a few dollars of the trigger.
payload.timing_relevance: High: this is a short-dated threshold-touch market, so residual path risk over the remaining days is the key variable.
unresolved_ambiguities: Future realized volatility over the remaining window is uncertain.
The exact path dependence of whether Binance prints 2400 before Apr 19 remains unresolved.
payload.what_would_change_view: I would move higher on renewed Binance momentum into 2397-2399 or a broad crypto risk-on move, and lower on repeated rejection below $2,400 or a pullback that moves ETH materially away from the threshold.
